verdict.answer_first: A opens with the actionable definition and why it matters: "four guarantees... so your data stays correct even when things go wrong"; B also answers early but adds more framing.
verdict.decisiveness: A gives a clearer rule: "Reach for it whenever incorrect data would actually hurt"; B surveys tradeoffs and offers follow-up depth.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on right now because it gives the core definition, practical examples, when to use ACID, and when it matters less without extra detours.
